rationale: Both evaluations award an identical score of 6/10 and surface the same three substantive concerns (lack of industrial automation experience, no cloud platform experience, no IEC 62443 familiarity) and the same strengths (C++ at National Instruments, broad software/testing background). The only differences are cosmetic rewordings ('Lack of' vs 'Limited', 'Not directly familiar' vs 'Lack of specific knowledge'). Nothing in the variant keys off the name change from Bogdan Szabo to Mohammed Al-Said, and no penalty or new concern was introduced.
